mnemonics.title: I B U - Induction Three Steps
mnemonics.content:
I = Inductive Hypothesis (Assume true for k)
B = Base Case (Prove for n=1)
U = Inductive Step (Prove true for k+1)

mnemonics.title: P.I.P. - Proof Inductive Principle
mnemonics.content:
P = Prove Base Case (n=1)
I = Inductive Hypothesis (Assume P(k))
P = Prove for k+1 using P(k)

mnemonics.title: Strong vs Weak - S.W.A.P
mnemonics.content:
S = Strong: Assume 1 through k
W = Weak: Assume just k
A = Always equivalent
P = Pick whichever is easier

acronyms.usage: The assumption that P(k) is true used to prove P(k+1)
The initial proof that establishes P(1) or starting value is true
The proof that P(k) implies P(k+1)
Condition true before and after each iteration of a loop

visualTips.visualization: Imagine dominos set up in a line. Base case knocks down the first domino (proves n=1). Each domino knocking down the next represents P(k) → P(k+1). Once started, all dominos fall.
The base case is your first foot on the ladder. The inductive step is the belief that if you're at any rung k, you can reach k+1. Thus you can climb infinitely.
Like building a tower - each floor depends not just on the floor below but all floors below it. You need to ensure the entire foundation is solid.
Like proving a property of all members of a family tree - prove it for the ancestors (base cases), then prove if parents have the property, children do too.

analogies.analogy: The foundation of a building - without a solid foundation, the entire structure will collapse regardless of how well the upper floors are built.
The chain in a chain reaction - each step triggers the next. If one link fails, the entire chain breaks.
Dominoes falling - knock the first one over (base case), and each domino knocks over the next (inductive step), causing the entire chain to fall.
Building a team reputation - it's not just dependent on the last person who joined, but on all previous members' contributions.
The rules of a game that remain constant throughout play - before each move, during each move, and after the game ends, the rules are still valid.
